name: Yahoo Finance: News Headlines
description: Fetch the latest Yahoo Finance news headlines for a ticker. Returns headline, URL, publisher, and relative timestamp. Pure read; complements finance/stock-price (quote) since news is a separate output contract.

help.summary: Latest Yahoo Finance news headlines for a given ticker.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Same ticker grammar as finance/stock-price (AAPL, ^GSPC, BTC-USD, EURUSD=X, BMW.DE, etc.).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of headlines to return. Default 20; capped at the number Yahoo renders without scrolling (typically 20–25).

examples.command: rzn-browser run finance yahoo-news --param ticker="^GSPC" --param limit="5"

help.returns: Object: { ticker, count, headlines: [{ title, url, publisher, age }] }. Pair with finance/stock-price for current quote on the same ticker.
notes: JS-first: reads [data-testid='storyitem']. Yahoo has used this contract for several redesigns.
Publisher and timestamp fields can come back as 'N/A' on some story types (sponsored content, video-only items).

browser_automation.description: Workflow to extract latest news headlines from finance.yahoo.com/quote/<ticker>/news.

sequences.name: yahoo_news_extract
sequences.description: Navigate to the news tab for a ticker; read story items via JS.

steps.name: Navigate to Yahoo news for ticker
Extract news headlines
steps.script: const cleanArg = (v) => { const t = String(v == null ? '' : v).trim(); return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t) ? '' : t; }; const ticker = cleanArg(arg0); if (!ticker) throw new Error('ticker is required'); const limitRaw = cleanArg(arg1); const limit = limitRaw && /^[1-9][0-9]*$/.test(limitRaw) ? parseInt(limitRaw, 10) : 20; const norm = (v) => { const t = String(v || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(); return t || 'N/A'; }; const items = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("[data-testid='storyitem']")); if (!items.length) throw new Error('No story items found for ' + ticker); const headlines = items.slice(0, limit).map((item) => { const link = item.querySelector('a[href]'); const headlineText = item.querySelector('h3, h4, [class*="headline"]')?.textContent || link?.textContent || ''; const publisher = item.querySelector('[class*="publisher"], [class*="source"], [data-testid*="publisher"]')?.textContent || ''; const age = item.querySelector('time, [class*="timestamp"]')?.textContent || ''; return { title: norm(headlineText), url: link?.href || 'N/A', publisher: norm(publisher), age: norm(age) }; }).filter((h) => h.title !== 'N/A'); return { ticker, count: headlines.length, headlines };
